name: Public Sector & Government Agencies in Germany
description: Federal, state (Länder), and municipal government agencies and public-sector institutions in Germany, including ministries, federal agencies (Bundesbehörden), state administrations, public-law institutions, and large municipal IT service providers (e.g., Dataport, IT.NRW). This segment is driven by the Onlinezugangsgesetz (OZG) digitalisation mandate, the German government's multi-cloud strategy (Deutsche Verwaltungscloud), and strict sovereignty requirements mandating BSI C5 Type 2-certified, EU-operated infrastructure.

decision_drivers: BSI C5 Type 2 certification (mandatory for federal cloud)
Digital sovereignty — no US CLOUD Act exposure
Long procurement cycles via EVB-IT / framework agreements

sizing.methodology: LLM estimation based on Bitkom public-sector IT studies, BMI budget publications, and ISG Provider Lens for German public cloud as of early 2026

tam.label: Total Addressable Market
tam.description: Total cloud and managed-infrastructure spend across German public-sector entities (federal, state, municipal). Includes IaaS, PaaS, sovereign cloud, managed services, and migration. Germany's public-sector IT market is ~EUR 40B total; cloud-relevant infrastructure is estimated at ~EUR 6B and growing rapidly due to OZG and Verwaltungscloud mandates.

sam.label: Serviceable Addressable Market
sam.description: Share reachable by T-Systems given its sovereign cloud (BSI C5 certified), Open Sovereign Cloud partnership with Google, and existing framework-agreement positions. Approximately 33% of TAM, excluding segments locked into in-house IT centres or competitors with existing long-term contracts.

som.label: Serviceable Obtainable Market
som.description: Realistic 3-year capture reflecting T-Systems' strong positioning: BSI C5 Type 2 certification, Deutsche Telekom government relationships, Open Sovereign Cloud (with Google), and Telekom's existing public-sector framework agreements. Assumes ~17.5% capture of SAM, accounting for competition from Ionos, SAP Sovereign Cloud, and public-sector IT consolidators.
